---
title: "DM_R009: Special ID Conventions"
subtitle: "Special category IDs must follow consistent conventions across the system, with reserved patterns for meta-categories and special values to ensure reliable identification and processing"
chapter: "CH02"
category: "rule"
number: "DM_R009"
date-created: "2025-08-18"
date-modified: "2025-08-18"
author: "Claude"
type: "rule"
law: "Data Management Law"
article-number: 
  number: 9
  title: "Special ID Convention Rules"
related_to:
  - "MP034: Special Treatment of ALL Category"
  - "DM_P003: Dual ID Format"
  - "UI_R008: Default Selection Conventions"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Article 9: Special ID Convention Rules {#overview}

**Data Management Law Article 9 (Special ID Convention Rules)**

This rule establishes consistent conventions for special category identifiers throughout the system.

:::{.callout-important}
## Core Rule
Special category IDs must follow consistent conventions across the system, with reserved patterns for meta-categories and special values to ensure reliable identification and processing.
:::

# Section 1: Reserved ID Patterns {#reserved-patterns}

## Subsection 1: Meta-Categories {#meta-categories}

1. **ALL Category**: `"000"` or `"all"`
   - Represents all available products
   - Used for "show everything" options
   - Example: "All Products", "All Platforms"

2. **None/Not Applicable**: `"999"` or `"none"`
   - Represents absence of a categorization
   - Used when product doesn't fit any defined category
   - Example: "Uncategorized", "Other", "N/A"

3. **Multiple Selection**: `"998"` or `"multiple"`
   - Represents multiple selected products
   - Used in summary views when multiple products are selected
   - Example: "Multiple Selections (3 products)"

4. **Unknown/Unspecified**: `"997"` or `"unknown"`
   - Represents missing or unknown information
   - Used when category information is truly unknown
   - Example: "Unknown Source", "Unspecified Category"

## Subsection 2: ID Formatting {#id-formatting}

1. **Numeric IDs**
   - Standard categories: `"001"` through `"996"`
   - Category IDs MUST be zero-padded to ensure consistent length
   - For hierarchical categories, use dot notation: `"001.002.003"`

2. **String IDs**
   - Use lowercase with underscores: `"category_name"`
   - For special categories, use prefixes:
     - `"all_"`
     - `"other_"`
     - `"unknown_"`

3. **Compound IDs**
   - Format: `"[type]__[id]"`
   - Example: `"product__001"`, `"platform__002"`
   - Reserved metadata prefix: `"__meta__"`

# Section 2: Implementation Guidelines {#implementation}

## Subsection 1: Database Storage {#database-storage}

```sql
-- Include special category ALL in dimension table
INSERT INTO product_categories (id, name_en, name_zh) VALUES ('000', 'All Products', '所有產品');

-- Include special category OTHER in dimension table
INSERT INTO product_categories (id, name_en, name_zh) VALUES ('999', 'Other Products', '其他產品');
```

## Subsection 2: Code Usage {#code-usage}

```r
# Define constants for special IDs
ALL_CATEGORY_ID <- "000"
OTHER_CATEGORY_ID <- "999"
MULTIPLE_SELECTION_ID <- "998"
UNKNOWN_CATEGORY_ID <- "997"

# Special case handling in filtering logic
filter_products <- function(category_id, products) {
  if (category_id == ALL_CATEGORY_ID) {
    return(products)  # Return all products without filtering
  } else if (category_id == OTHER_CATEGORY_ID) {
    return(products[is.na(products$category_id) | products$category_id == "", ])
  } else {
    return(products[products$category_id == category_id, ])
  }
}
```

## Subsection 3: Excel Parameter Files {#excel-parameters}

```
# First row in product_line.xlsx:
| id   | name_en      | name_zh   |
| "000" | "All Products" | "所有產品" |

# Last row in product_line.xlsx:
| id   | name_en      | name_zh   |
| "999" | "Other Products" | "其他產品" |
```

# Section 3: Implementation Examples {#examples}

## Subsection 1: UI Component Integration {#ui-integration}

```r
# Using special IDs in dropdown menus
pickerInput(
  inputId = "product_filter",
  label = "Select Product",
  choices = list(
    "All Products" = ALL_CATEGORY_ID,
    "Product A" = "001",
    "Product B" = "002",
    "Other Products" = OTHER_CATEGORY_ID
  ),
  selected = ALL_CATEGORY_ID  # Default to "All"
)
```

## Subsection 2: Data Processing Logic {#data-processing}

```r
# Handle special categories in data aggregation
aggregate_by_category <- function(data, category_id) {
  if (category_id == ALL_CATEGORY_ID) {
    # Aggregate all data
    result <- data %>%
      summarise(
        total = sum(value, na.rm = TRUE),
        count = n()
      )
  } else if (category_id == MULTIPLE_SELECTION_ID) {
    # Handle multiple selections
    result <- data %>%
      filter(category_id %in% selected_categories) %>%
      summarise(
        total = sum(value, na.rm = TRUE),
        count = n()
      )
  } else {
    # Standard category processing
    result <- data %>%
      filter(category_id == !!category_id) %>%
      summarise(
        total = sum(value, na.rm = TRUE),
        count = n()
      )
  }
  return(result)
}
```

# Section 4: Benefits {#benefits}

Following this rule will:

1. **Consistent Processing**: Enables reliable special case handling
2. **Clear Semantics**: Makes code more readable with obvious special cases
3. **Query Optimization**: Allows efficient database queries for common cases
4. **Sort Order**: Ensures predictable sorting in UI components
5. **Translation Support**: Special categories can be translated like regular ones

# Section 5: Validation Checklist {#validation-checklist}

When implementing special ID conventions:

1. ☐ Define all special ID constants
2. ☐ Create special category records in database
3. ☐ Implement special case handling logic
4. ☐ Update Excel parameter files
5. ☐ Configure UI component defaults
6. ☐ Test filtering and aggregation logic
7. ☐ Verify sort order
8. ☐ Confirm translation support

---

*Based on: Data Management Law Article 9*  
*Related to: [MP034 Special Treatment of ALL Category](../../CH00_fundamental_principles/meta_principles/MP034_special_treatment_all.qmd), [UI_R008 Default Selection Conventions](../../CH04_ui_components/rules/UI_R008_default_selection_conventions.qmd)*
